title: pavonia speciosa subsp. polymorpha
intro: Pavonia speciosa subsp. polymorpha, also known as the rose mallow or pavonia, is a plant species in the mallow family that is native to Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, due to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of pavonia speciosa subsp. polymorpha include its use in treating wounds, fever, and digestive issues, as well as its potential anti-cancer and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
